name: Shrimp Stuffed Poblanos
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine queso fresco and corn in a food processor; process until corn is no longer visible. Transfer the cheese mixture to a large bowl.
2. Add rice, shrimp, Cheddar cheese, Monterey Jack cheese, onion, sour cream, and salt to the bowl with the cheese mixture. Set aside.
3. Cut a small slit in one side of each poblano pepper and carefully remove seeds, leaving stems intact. Pat peppers dry.
4. Stuff each pepper with approximately 1/4 cup of shrimp mixture. Secure with a wooden pick if necessary.
5. Combine bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, and melted butter in a medium bowl; stir until blended. Set aside.
6. Beat eggs with water in a medium bowl. Roll stuffed peppers in flour, dip in egg mixture, then coat with the bread crumb mixture.
7. Place peppers in a shallow baking dish, allowing space between each pepper. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes or until golden brown and thoroughly heated. Serve immediately.
Substitute a mixture of 1/2 cup cottage cheese and 1/2 cup feta cheese for the queso fresco, if desired.
